channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20039] sanity-lnet test_257: FAIL: Failed to enable routing rc = 244
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/94d57283-1fa2-4942-ab89-66b3a9b3db6d

test_257 failed with the following error:

Enable routing on onyx-180vm2
CMD: onyx-180vm2 /usr/sbin/lnetctl set routing 1
onyx-180vm2: add:
onyx-180vm2:     - routing:
onyx-180vm2:           errno: -12
onyx-180vm2:           descr: "cannot enable routing Cannot allocate memory"
pdsh@onyx-180vm1: onyx-180vm2: ssh exited with exit code 244
 sanity-lnet test_257: @@@@@@ FAIL: Failed to enable routing rc = 244

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4708 - 5.14.0-503.40.1.el9_5.aarch64+64k
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4708 - 4.18.0-553.89.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-lnet test_257 - Failed to enable routing rc = 244
item.summary: sanity-lnet test_257: FAIL: Failed to enable routing rc = 244

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65153
Subject: LU-20039 lnet: preserve router buffer sizing on re-enable
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 5bef6c575d2b8d79d6d55dfe9501cc80099b2f4a

The router is initially configured with small number of buffers:

38597.432010] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl set routing 1
[38597.560162] LNet: 818537:0:(router.c:1476:lnet_nrb_tiny_calculate()) tiny_router_buffers=9 less than recommended minimum 512
[38597.561704] LNet: 818537:0:(router.c:1498:lnet_nrb_small_calculate()) small_router_buffers=8 less than recommended minimum 4096
[38597.563327] LNet: 818537:0:(router.c:1520:lnet_nrb_large_calculate()) large_router_buffers=4 less than recommended minimum 256

But when routing is disabled and then re-enabled, it is trying to allocate a gig of large buffers:

38620.581700] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl set routing 0
[38620.729652] LNet: Message forwarding will stop in 10s to 30s
[38620.946750] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark Wait for lst to finish
[38621.125030] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: Wait for lst to finish
[38631.909347] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl net show -v; /usr/sbin/lnetctl peer show -v
[38632.253245] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark Wait 5s for LST to start
[38632.412432] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: Wait 5s for LST to start
[38632.901770] LNet: Message forwarding disabled
[38637.611743] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark Enable routing on onyx-180vm2
[38637.783325] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: Enable routing on onyx-180vm2
[38637.928726] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl set routing 1
[38638.127192] lnetctl: page allocation failure: order:0, mode:0x10dc0(GFP_KERNEL|__GFP_NORETRY|__GFP_ZERO), nodemask=(null),cpuset=/,mems_allowed=0
[38638.128940] CPU: 1 PID: 819427 Comm: lnetctl Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE     -------  ---  5.14.0-503.40.1.el9_5.aarch64+64k #1
[38638.130471] Hardware name: QEMU KVM Virtual Machine, BIOS edk2-20241117-4.el9 11/17/2024
[38638.131477] Call trace:
[38638.131804]  dump_backtrace+0xa8/0x120
[38638.132343]  show_stack+0x1c/0x30
[38638.132811]  dump_stack_lvl+0x74/0x8c
[38638.133329]  dump_stack+0x14/0x24
[38638.133797]  warn_alloc+0x118/0x1b0
[38638.134291]  __alloc_pages_slowpath.constprop.0+0x8d8/0x94c
[38638.135082]  __alloc_pages+0x234/0x290
[38638.135612]  lnet_new_rtrbuf+0x190/0x3fc [lnet]
[38638.136331]  lnet_rtrpool_adjust_bufs+0x11c/0x2a0 [lnet]
[38638.137104]  lnet_rtrpools_adjust_helper+0x130/0x164 [lnet]
[38638.137913]  lnet_rtrpools_enable+0x4c/0x1b0 [lnet]
[38638.138628]  LNetCtl+0x193c/0x1c50 [lnet]
[38638.139222]  lnet_ioctl+0x280/0x300 [lnet]
[38638.139828]  lnet_psdev_ioctl+0x394/0x500 [lnet]
[38638.140508]  __arm64_sys_ioctl+0xa8/0xec
[38638.141066]  invoke_syscall.constprop.0+0x7c/0xd0
[38638.141732]  do_el0_svc+0xb4/0xd0
[38638.142206]  el0_svc+0xf0/0x200
[38638.142653]  el0t_64_sync_handler+0x134/0x150
[38638.143263]  el0t_64_sync+0x17c/0x180
[38638.143839] Mem-Info:
[38638.144127] active_anon:2756 inactive_anon:40 isolated_anon:0
                active_file:5226 inactive_file:8319 isolated_file:0
                unevictable:0 dirty:1 writeback:0
                slab_reclaimable:793 slab_unreclaimable:1126
                mapped:685 shmem:352 pagetables:261
                sec_pagetables:0 bounce:0
                kernel_misc_reclaimable:0
                free:13358 free_pcp:14 free_cma:0
[38638.148842] Node 0 active_anon:173760kB inactive_anon:5184kB active_file:336064kB inactive_file:481216kB unevictable:0kB isolated(anon):0kB isolated(file):0kB mapped:43840kB dirty:64kB writeback:0kB shmem:22528kB shmem_thp: 0kB shmem_pmdmapped: 0kB anon_thp: 0kB writeback_tmp:0kB kernel_stack:9856kB pagetables:16704kB sec_pagetables:0kB all_unreclaimable? no
[38638.152904] Node 0 DMA free:873664kB boost:524288kB min:653632kB low:685952kB high:718272kB reserved_highatomic:0KB active_anon:79488kB inactive_anon:0kB active_file:164352kB inactive_file:364224kB unevictable:0kB writepending:64kB present:3145728kB managed:2605696kB mlocked:0kB bounce:0kB free_pcp:512kB local_pcp:0kB free_cma:0kB
[38638.156521] lowmem_reserve[]: 0 0 59 59 59
[38638.157117] Node 0 Normal free:70080kB boost:0kB min:48128kB low:60160kB high:72192kB reserved_highatomic:0KB active_anon:98944kB inactive_anon:320kB active_file:120192kB inactive_file:137536kB unevictable:0kB writepending:0kB present:1048576kB managed:996096kB mlocked:0kB bounce:0kB free_pcp:192kB local_pcp:0kB free_cma:0kB
[38638.160663] lowmem_reserve[]: 0 0 0 0 0
[38638.161148] Node 0 DMA: 1797*64kB (UME) 769*128kB (ME) 311*256kB (UME) 101*512kB (ME) 60*1024kB (UME) 29*2048kB (UME) 18*4096kB (UME) 11*8192kB (UME) 4*16384kB (UME) 1*32768kB (M) 1*65536kB (U) 1*131072kB (U) 0*262144kB 0*524288kB = 924352kB
[38638.164109] Node 0 Normal: 355*64kB (UM) 145*128kB (UM) 68*256kB (UM) 19*512kB (U) 3*1024kB (U) 1*2048kB (U) 0*4096kB 0*8192kB 0*16384kB 0*32768kB 0*65536kB 0*131072kB 0*262144kB 0*524288kB = 73536kB
[38638.166332] Node 0 hugepages_total=0 hugepages_free=0 hugepages_surp=0 hugepages_size=16777216kB
[38638.167417] Node 0 hugepages_total=0 hugepages_free=0 hugepages_surp=0 hugepages_size=524288kB
[38638.168619] Node 0 hugepages_total=0 hugepages_free=0 hugepages_surp=0 hugepages_size=2048kB
[38638.169749] 11936 total pagecache pages
[38638.170236] 0 pages in swap cache
[38638.170663] Free swap  = 3800896kB
[38638.171095] Total swap = 3801024kB
[38638.171528] 65536 pages RAM
[38638.171923] 0 pages HighMem/MovableOnly
[38638.172410] 9258 pages reserved
[38638.172837] 0 pages cma reserved
[38638.173263] 0 pages hwpoisoned
[38638.173656] LNetError: 819427:0:(router.c:1390:lnet_rtrpool_adjust_bufs()) lnet: error allocating 1024x16 page router buffers on CPT 0: rc = -12

in lnet/lnet/router.c:

  • lnet_rtrpools_disable() clears tiny_router_buffers, small_router_buffers, and large_router_buffers.
  • lnet_rtrpools_enable() rebuilds existing pools with lnet_rtrpools_adjust_helper(0, 0, 0), and 0 means “use default
    sizing,” so re-enable tries to allocate the large default pool set.
hornc, could you please take a look, I've seen this subtest fail a bunch of times now.
Test was added on 2025-08-26 in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/57490 ("LU-15135 lnet: Graceful router removal and addition").
This failed 9 times in the past 2 weeks on aarch64 clients.
